The MEURI framework for Ebola virus disease therapeutics

Tables and Figures

Overview

Tables 1-4, Figures 1-4 (main text) and Tables S1-S4, Figures S1-S6 (supplement), from meuri_clean.csv (n = 756). PNGs saved to tables/ and figures/.

Balance of prognostic factors across treatment groups

Prognostic-factor balance across treatment groups (descriptive)
n Ct-NP, median (IQR) Days onset–treatment, median (IQR) Vaccinated, %
mAb114 252 24.3 (20.5–29.2) 5 (3–7) 11.5
REGN-EB3 232 23.7 (20.3–29.6) 5 (3–7) 12.1
Remdesivir 221 24.4 (20.9–28.5) 6 (4–8) 13.6
ZMapp 51 22.8 (20.0–25.9) 5 (4–8) 11.8
Descriptive comparison only — no formal balance test applied (none was specified in the material provided).

Marginal standardization / adjusted CFR (Table 4)

\[\text{logit}\big(\Pr(\text{Death}_i = 1)\big) = \beta_0 + \beta_{1}\,\text{Treatment}_i + X_i\boldsymbol{\beta}\]

Where \(X_i\boldsymbol{\beta}\) represents the fully adjusted linear predictor: \[\begin{aligned} X_i\boldsymbol{\beta} = {} & \beta_{2}\,\text{AgeGroup}_i + \beta_{3}\,\text{Sex}_i + \beta_{4}\,\text{Hospital}_i + f_{\text{rcs}}(\text{CalendarTime}_i) \\ & + \beta_{5}\,\text{Ct-NP}_i + \beta_{6}\,\text{Delay}_i + \beta_{7}\,\text{Vaccine}_i \end{aligned}\]

\[\widehat{\text{CFR}}_{\text{adj}}(t) = \frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^{n} \widehat{\Pr}\big(\text{Death}_i = 1 \mid \text{Treatment} = t,\, X_i\big)\]

Table 4. Unadjusted vs. marginally-standardized CFR by treatment
Unadjusted CFR % (95% CI) Standardized CFR % (95% CI)
mAb114 31.9 (26.4, 37.9) 32.7 (27.1, 38.8)
REGN-EB3 31.3 (25.7, 37.6) 31.8 (26.2, 37.2)
Remdesivir 51.1 (44.6, 57.6) 49.8 (43.6, 55.5)
ZMapp 47.1 (34.1, 60.5) 44.1 (31.6, 55.2)
Standardized to the covariate distribution of the full analysis sample via g-computation; 95% CI from 500 nonparametric bootstrap replicates.

Validation vs. published Table 4:

Model validation: reproduced vs. published adjusted CFR
Published Table 4 (%) This model (%) Difference (pp)
mAb114 32.7 32.7 +0.02
REGN-EB3 31.8 31.8 +0.00
Remdesivir 49.8 49.8 -0.03
ZMapp 44.1 44.1 -0.01
Differences under ~0.1 percentage points across all four treatments confirm the covariate set above.
